assertion_results.text: Output must address ownership ambiguity explicitly
assertion_results.evidence: Line 9: 'Leadership instability (3 VPs in 18 months) and a 6-month unresolved outage signal deep ownership failure.' Line 19 scores 'Ownership & culture 1/5 — No product owner, revolving VP, 4 engineers on loan.' Ownership ambiguity is named and scored explicitly.

assertion_results.text: Output must mention both CI systems by name (CircleCI and GitHub Actions)
assertion_results.evidence: Line 7: 'CI is split across CircleCI and GitHub Actions with no authoritative deployment owner.' Both names also appear in the blocker section (line 22): 'Dual CI ambiguity (CircleCI vs GitHub Actions)' and in action item A1.

assertion_results.text: Output must give at least one short-term action item that is actionable despite the chaos
assertion_results.evidence: Short term section lists four concrete actions, e.g. 'Declare one authoritative CI/CD pipeline: pick either CircleCI or GitHub Actions (GitHub Actions recommended)' and 'Run the full test suite and triage results'. Action items A1–A4 are all tagged short-term with named owners.

assertion_results.text: Output must score Ownership & culture at 2/5 or lower
assertion_results.evidence: Scoring table row: 'Ownership & culture | 1/5 | No product owner, revolving VP, 4 engineers on loan.' 1/5 is at or below the 2/5 threshold.

assertion_results.text: Output must not recommend a full team reorganisation
assertion_results.evidence: The report recommends 'Assign a temporary product owner or rotating tech lead to fill the ownership vacuum' (A7) and 'Resolve on-loan headcount: return engineers or re-scope team charter' (A10). No recommendation to restructure team topology or reorganise the entire organisation is present.
